memoryArtifacts.headline: King Edward I and Eleanor of Castille

16th-century illustration of Edward I presiding over Parliament. The scene shows Alexander III of Scotland and Llywelyn ap Gruffudd of Wales on either side of Edward; an episode that never actually occurred.
Edward I (right) giving homage to Philip IV (left). As Duke of Aquitaine, Edward was a vassal of the French king.

popularStory.headline: Bio of King Edward Longshanks I (entered by Michael A. Kennedy)
popularStory.description: Edward I (17/18 June 1239 – 7 July 1307), also known as Edward Longshanks and the Hammer of the Scots Malleus Scotorum, was King of England from 1272 to 1307. The first son of Henry III of England|Hen …

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Edward I (17/18 June 1239 – 7 July 1307), also known as Edward Longshanks and the Hammer of the Scots (Latin: Malleus Scotorum), was King of England from 1272 to 1307.
...
First marriage

By his first wife Eleanor of Castile, Edward had at least fourteen children, perhaps as many as sixteen. Of these, five daughters survived into adulthood, but only one son outlived his father, becoming King Edward II (1307–1327). He was reportedly concerned with his son's failure to live up to the expectations of an heir to the crown, and at one point decided to exile the prince's favourite Piers Gaveston.

Edward's children with Eleanor were:
1. Katherine (before 17 June 1264 – 5 September 1264), buried at Westminster Abbey.
2. Joanna (Summer or January 1265 – before 7 September 1265), buried in Westminster Abbey.
3. John (13 July 1266 – 3 August 1271), predeceased his father and died at Wallingford while in the custody of his granduncle Richard, Earl of Cornwall; buried at Westminster Abbey.
4. Henry (6 May 1268 – 14 October 1274), predeceased his father, buried in Westminster Abbey.
5. Eleanor (c. 18 June 1269 – 19 August 1298); in 1293 she married Henry III, Count of Bar, by whom she had two children, buried in Westminster Abbey.
6. Juliana (after May 1271 – 5 September 1271), born and died while Edward and Eleanor were in Acre.
7. Joan of Acre (1272 – 23 April 1307), married (1) in 1290 Gilbert de Clare, 6th Earl of Hertford, who died in 1295, and (2) in 1297 Ralph de Monthermer. She had four children by Clare, and three or four by Monthermer.
8. Alphonso, Earl of Chester (24 November 1273 – 19 August 1284), predeceased his father, buried in Westminster Abbey.
9. Margaret (c.15 March 1275 – after 11 March 1333), married John II of Brabant in 1290, with whom she had one son.
10. Berengaria (May 1276 – between 7 June 1277 and 1278), buried in Westminster Abbey.
11. Daughter (December 1277 – January 1278), buried in Westminster Abbey.
12. Mary of Woodstock (11 March 1278 – before 8 July 1332[260]), a Benedictine nun in Amesbury Priory, Wiltshire, where she was probably buried.
13. Son (1280/81 – 1280/81), predeceased his father; little evidence exists for this child.
14. Elizabeth of Rhuddlan (c. 7 August 1282 – 5 May 1316), married (1) in 1297 John I, Count of Holland, (2) in 1302 Humphrey de Bohun, 4th Earl of Hereford. The first marriage was childless; by Bohun she had ten children.
15. Edward II (25 April 1284 – 21 September 1327), succeeded his father as king of England. In 1308 he married Isabella of France, with whom he had four children.

Second marriage
By Margaret of France, Edward had two sons, both of whom lived to adulthood, and a daughter who died as a child. The Hailes Abbey chronicle indicates that John Botetourt may have been Edward's illegitimate son; however, the claim is unsubstantiated.

His progeny by Margaret of France were:
1. Thomas of Brotherton, 1st Earl of Norfolk (1 June 1300 – 4 August 1338), buried in Bury St Edmunds Abbey. Married (1) Alice Hales, with issue; (2) Mary Brewes, no issue.
2. Edmund of Woodstock, 1st Earl of Kent (5 August 1301 – 19 March 1330), married Margaret Wake, 3rd Baroness Wake of Liddell, with issue.
3. Eleanor (4 May 1306 – August 1311).

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Edward_I_of_England

sourceList.title: Edward I, "United States, Wikipedia, Prominent Persons Index, 1500-2022"

zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward I King of England於1239年6月17日出生在英國米德萊可維斯特明斯特帕拉斯 本 維斯特明斯特時，他的父親Henry III King of England31歲，母親Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England18歲。他於1254年10月18日與Eleanor of Castile Queen of England在英國英格蘭倫敦維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和11個女兒的父母。他於1266年被列為high sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire。他去世於1307年7月7日在英國波藍德卡萊爾享年68歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Duarte
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward I King of England was born on 17 June 1239, in Palace of Westminster, Westminster, Middlesex, England, his father, Henry III King of England, was 31 and his mother, Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England, was 18. He married Eleanor of Castile Queen of England on 18 October 1254, in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 11 daughters. In 1266, his occupation is listed as high sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire. He died on 7 July 1307, in Carlisle, Cumberland, England, at the age of 68, and was buried in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward I King of England est né le 17 juin 1239, à Palace of Westminster, Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, Henry III King of England, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Eleanor of Castile Queen of England le 18 octobre 1254, à Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 11 filles. En 1266, sa profession de son indiquée est high sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire. Il est décédé le 7 juillet 1307, à Carlisle, Cumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterré à Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward I King of England am 17. Juni 1239 in Palace of Westminster, Westminster, Middlesex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry III King of England 31 und seine Mutter Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England 18. Er hat am 18. Oktober 1254 in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom Eleanor of Castile Queen of England geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 11 Töchter. 1266 ist sein Beruf als high sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire aufgeführt. Er starb am 7. Juli 1307 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Carlisle, Cumberland, England und wurde in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward I King of England è nato il il 17 giugno 1239 nel Palace of Westminster, Westminster, Middlesex, England, il suo padre, Henry III King of England, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Eleanor of Castile Queen of England il 18 ottobre 1254, nel Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 11 figlie. Nel 1266, la sua occupazione è indicata come high sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire. Morì il 7 luglio 1307, nel Carlisle, Cumberland, England, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolto a Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward I King of Englandが1239年6月17日にイギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスタープアレイス オフ ウエストミンスターで生まれた時，父Henry III King of Englandは31歳，母Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of Englandは18歳であった。1254年10月18日にイギリスイギリスロンドンウエストミンスターウエストミンスター アビーでEleanor of Castile Queen of Englandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と11人の娘の親であった。1266年，職業はhigh sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshireと記録されている。彼は1307年7月7日にイギリスクウンバーランドカーリスルで68歳で死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスターウエストミンスター アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London,: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이
ko.Palace of Westminster, Westminster, Midd: 영국 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 팔레이스 프 웨스트민스터
ko.lifeSummary: Edward I King of England(이)가 1239년 6월 17일 영국 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 팔레이스 프 웨스트민스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry III King of England(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1254년 10월 18일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이에서 Eleanor of Castile Queen of England(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 열 명 이상의 딸을 두었다. 1266년, 그의 직업은 high sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire이었다. 그는 1307년 7월 7일 영국 쿠벌랜드 칼리슬일에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Eleanor of Castile Queen of England em 18 de outubro de 1254, em Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 11 filhas. Em 1266, sua profissão aparece como high sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire. Ele faleceu em 7 de julho de 1307, em Carlisle, Cumberland, Inglaterra, com 68 anos, e foi sepultado em Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward I King of England родился 17 июня 1239 г. в местности палеис оф вестминстир, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry III King of England было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor of Castile Queen of England 18 октября 1254 г. в местности вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 11 дочерей. В 1266 году он работал по профессии high sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire. Он умер 7 июля 1307 г. в местности карлисл, кумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward I King of England nació el 17 de junio de 1239, en Palace of Westminster, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry III King of England, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Eleanor of Castile Queen of England el 18 de octubre de 1254, en Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 11 hijas. En 1266, su ocupación registrada era high sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire. Murió el 7 de julio de 1307, en Carlisle, Cumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultado en Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
